acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Migration tests cover multiple representative old-store fixtures beyond the minimal v0.1.26 case
- [ ] Coverage includes tombstones, deleted deps, multiple embedded notes, closed/claimed beads, and legacy label variants
- [ ] Each fixture runs through detect plus migrate-to-1 and verifies strict-load success after rewrite
- [ ] Fixture provenance and intended scenario coverage are documented
description: The current migration compatibility gate proves the exact minimal v0.1.26 wire boundary we were most worried about, but it does not cover the full range of old-store variants users may have in the wild. In particular, we still lack representative fixtures for richer v0.1.x stores with multiple beads, tombstones, mixed workflow/claim states, multiple notes, deleted deps, and other legacy field combinations that may have accumulated before the hard cutover.
design: Build a small matrix of source-locked old-store fixtures derived from historical v0.1.x wire shapes, then run current migrate detect and migrate to 1 against each fixture in integration tests. Cover representative real-world combinations rather than every combinatorial possibility: active and deleted deps, tombstones, closed and claimed beads, multiple embedded notes, legacy labels arrays, and stores with local/remote divergence. Keep fixtures auditable with provenance notes and a reproducible generator path where practical.
title: Expand migration coverage for real-world v0.1.x store variants
